title: cyanea parvifolia
intro: Cyanea parvifolia, also known as the Hawaiian hibiscus or na'u, is a flowering plant native to the Hawaiian Islands. The plant has been used in traditional Hawaiian medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The leaves and flowers of Cyanea parvifolia have been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, sore throats, and digestive issues, and are believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water or by using the flowers as a tea, and have been used to make topical creams and ointments for skin conditions.
